observed_evidence: Static review only: the checked-in core, references, helper scripts, validator suite, and two fresh-eye subagent passes were inspected. `achieve` was not executed end-to-end because `/goal` activation is host-driven and was not run, so no live-run proof is claimed.

Issue #223 (2026-05-27): the duplicate-test-pressure contract additions (Before-phase expected proof cost / test-duplication pressure, the During-phase cheap `--test-pressure` slice sample carried forward across compaction, and the After-phase new-slice-local vs accumulated-suite-debt classification) were reviewed by a 3-angle + counterweight fresh-eye critique. The new `Test duplication pressure` slice field is a backward-compatible additive label (no REQUIRED_SECTIONS change) guarded by `test_render_slice_block_includes_test_duplication_pressure`.

The checked-in `achieve` core Guardrails forbid generic task-runner behavior, auto-start before activation, handoff-as-running-scratchpad, broad gates after every small commit, and claiming live/provider proof when only local checks ran, which matches the design non-goals.

The goal artifact is created at `Status: draft` with an explicit `/goal @...` activation line, and `upsert_goal.py` never overwrites an existing body (it only rewrites the `Status:` line and only when it changes), so a reviewable activation artifact is produced rather than a runaway goal.

Metrics are represented honestly as host-agnostic shallow signals via `probe_host_logs.py` with deep Codex audit best-effort and `Metrics: when available`, instead of fabricating token or tool-call totals the host log does not expose.

The cross-skill behavior is owned by a presence-gated shared reference (`skills/shared/references/active-goal-coordination.md`) cited by impl and issue, with quality/critique cadence coordinated by achieve through the same reference; it is a silent no-op when no active goal artifact exists, so each coordinated skill stays useful standalone.

Two fresh-eye subagent passes found no correctness blockers in the four `achieve` scripts or the goal artifact helpers; slice append produces no trailing whitespace or doubled blank lines and `check_goal_artifact.py` passes on a scaffolded artifact.

Issue #233 (2026-05-29): the After-phase closeout-evidence gate gained F1 context binding — a cited `Retro:`/`Host log probe:` file must now bind to the goal (its basename or content references the goal slug or issue cluster parsed from the `Activation:` line), so a closeout can no longer pass by citing an unrelated pre-existing artifact (the demonstrated F1 attack). Reviewed by a 3-angle + counterweight fresh-eye critique that caught and closed a fail-open bypass (a bare `Activation:` substring yielding no tokens) and anchored the numeric token against false digit-run matches. F2 narration is surfaced as a non-blocking `narration_required_sections` affordance only; hard narration enforcement is deliberately NOT delivered (a transcript gate is judgment-bound and would over-fire) and remains open. Routing/draft-save behavior in this case is unchanged by the fix. Guarded by tests in tests/quality_gates/test_goal_artifact_lib.py and test_prescribed_skill_executed.py.

Coordination cues (2026-05-30): the goal artifact gained a `## Coordination Cues` carrier that defers phase routing to find-skills (no inline phase→skill map), plus two presence-only After-phase closeout floors — gather (external source in `## Context Sources`) and release (release-surface token in recorded work) — mirroring the #253 disposition floor (clone-safe, `## Coordination Cues`-scoped, `Created`-grandfathered ≥ 2026-05-31). The dogfood acceptance evidence (routes to `achieve`, saves a draft goal with an explicit `/goal @...` line, stays inert until activation, no auto-execute) is UNCHANGED by these additive closeout-side gates. Reviewed by a fresh-eye opus implementation critique (subagent a1052f6babb139f00) that found no blockers and caught one order-dependent false-refusal (N1, fixed with a regression test); guarded by 25 tests in tests/quality_gates/test_goal_coordination_floors.py with the full suite green. Also covers the same-session verify→publish lifecycle tightening (commit-time gate aggregate is a distinct verification surface from `pytest`; reactive 'run the aggregate on first rejection' trigger added to references/lifecycle.md). Routing/draft-save behavior unchanged.

Issue #268 (2026-05-31): host-level completion is now explicitly downstream of the durable artifact closeout: before any host status tool marks a goal complete, the checked-in goal must already read `Status: complete` and pass `check_goal_artifact.py --goal-path <artifact>`. This preserves the existing dogfood contract (draft artifact is inert until `/goal`; `/goal` pursues only) while preventing host green status from substituting for the artifact evidence floor. Guarded by `test_host_goal_completion_is_downstream_of_artifact_closeout`; no maintained Cautilus scenario change because routing and draft-save behavior are unchanged.

Issue #269 (2026-05-31): `check_goal_artifact.py` now adds a mutable-HEAD freshness report that rejects current-HEAD prose naming a non-HEAD immutable SHA unless the line marks the SHA as historical/proof-targeted or records a live `--head-sha HEAD` command. This adds closeout honesty teeth without changing achieve routing, draft creation, activation, or host-adapter behavior. Guarded by `tests/quality_gates/test_goal_head_freshness.py`; maintained Cautilus scenario unchanged.

Issue #237 (2026-06-01): `achieve` After-phase guidance now requires live apply/checkpoint closeouts to record the applied checkpoint, current `HEAD`, and classify post-checkpoint commits as `runtime-affecting`, `test-only`, or `audit-doc-only`, with uncertainty treated as runtime-affecting. This preserves the existing goal lifecycle contract while making `HEAD != live` explainable without forcing blind re-apply. Guarded by tests/quality_gates/test_workflow_safety_docs.py.

Phase-routing floor (2026-06-04): `achieve` closeout now refuses recorded implementation/debug/quality/issue work that lacks a `Routing:` line naming `find-skills` plus the routed skill, or a long explicit opt-out. This is an additive closeout-side evidence floor: it proves owner-skill boundaries were considered, while `find-skills` still owns the actual recommendation and the maintained consumer prompt still routes to `achieve`, saves an inert draft artifact, and waits for `/goal` activation. Guarded by tests/quality_gates/test_goal_coordination_floors.py plus a fresh-eye review that fixed stale closeout docs and a `regression suite passed` false-positive.

Workflow-review cadence (2026-06-02): the generated active frame and lifecycle now make fresh-eye critique a slice/bundle risk-boundary proof instead of a per-commit ritual, and the slice packet explicitly names changed files plus owning/generated surfaces, invariants, proof, non-claims, out-of-scope lines, and reviewer questions. This preserves the existing `achieve` dogfood contract: routing, draft artifact creation, activation, and inert-until-activation behavior are unchanged. Guarded by tests/quality_gates/test_goal_artifact_lib.py and tests/quality_gates/test_critique_skill.py.

Issue #279 (2026-06-02): `achieve` activation-discussion closeout first made the human-facing warning explicit: `discussion_summary_present` only proves the decision was surfaced, not resolved, and operators must resolve or explicitly ask before offering activation. Issue #291 (2026-06-04) then tightened the machine contract: `check_goal_artifact.py --pursue-ready` now separates `shape_ready` from `activation_ready`, returns nonzero with `pursue_ready: false` while consequential discussion is only surfaced, and passes only when the summary is explicitly marked resolved/confirmed/approved. This preserves `/goal` as pure pursue while preventing warning-only activation. Guarded by tests/quality_gates/test_goal_artifact_lib.py, tests/quality_gates/test_achieve_before_activation.py, and tests/charness_cli/test_goal_helpers.py; fresh-eye causal review found the old consumer-boundary failure and the debug artifact records the RCA.

Issue #282 (2026-06-03): the After-phase now records the goal window deterministically (`record_metric_window.py` writes an idempotent `Host metric window:` line under `## Final Verification`, extracted to `goal_metric_window_lib.py` to keep `goal_artifact_lib.py` under its code-line gate), `check_goal_artifact.py` surfaces a non-blocking `closeout_evidence.metric_window` signal (recorded/incomplete/absent) so a forgotten window is visible at the gate instead of silently reported thread-wide, and the standardized provider-safe measured-vs-proxy closeout block renders via `probe_host_logs.py --goal-path <artifact> --format markdown`. A broad-gate attestation hook (`goal_metrics_render_lib.render_broad_gate_attestation`) records gate results (gate/outcome/state_ref) with no command field, so closeout evidence cannot re-advertise provider CLI command strings. Additive closeout-side behavior; routing, draft creation, activation, and inert-until-activation are unchanged, and the window signal never gates the flip (a host lacking timestamps records `unavailable`). Reviewed by a fresh-eye resolution critique (SHIP-WITH-FIXES; the recurrence blocker was folded into the non-blocking closeout signal); deterministic gates own closeout (cautilus run_mode: ask, next_action: none). Guarded by tests/quality_gates/test_goal_metrics_render.py and tests/quality_gates/test_record_metric_window.py.

Issue #287 (2026-06-04): `achieve` now ships an adapter seam for closeout publication and Auto-Retro disposition policy. Missing adapters resolve to audit-only defaults, repo adapters can declare handoff-only or publish-capable defaults plus a direct-commit issue-closeout carrier, and `check_goal_artifact.py` surfaces `achieve_adapter_policy` during complete-state evidence while blocking found invalid adapters. Scenario review kept the maintained consumer prompt unchanged because deterministic coverage owns resolver parsing, missing-adapter fallback, direct-commit rehearsal binding, and complete-evidence integration in tests/quality_gates/test_achieve_adapter_policy.py.

Reference-index cleanup (2026-06-04): `achieve` now lists `references/index.md` from the root surface and keeps the maintained consumer contract unchanged: the prompt still routes to `achieve`, saves an inert draft goal artifact, and waits for `/goal` activation. The semantic change is discoverability/readability only; deterministic tests and validators prove that index bullet items preserve reference listing coverage without treating incidental prose as a listing.

Issue #298 (2026-06-04): `achieve` lifecycle guidance now separates issue-resolution carrier publication from later lifecycle/audit artifact publication, so a verified direct-commit issue carrier does not force a second docs-only issue-closeout push for goal, retro, or handoff refreshes. Issue #359 (2026-06-13): `achieve` complete-state closeout now refuses section-level scaffold placeholders whose first body line still begins with a closeout-pending marker (`Pending until`, `TODO`, `TBD`, `FIXME`, `To be filled`, or a label-prefixed placeholder value such as `Retro dispositions: TODO`). Issue #336 (2026-06-08): the Before-phase now explicitly states it is artifact-only and must not consume the host active-goal slot — the symmetric counterpart of the #268 completion-downstream rule — with the slot consumed only at `/goal @...` pursuit. Guarded by `tests/quality_gates/test_achieve_before_activation.py::test_drafting_does_not_consume_host_goal_slot`.

Issue #385 (2026-06-18): `achieve` now refuses the `blocked` status flip until the goal classifies every external/live proof lane in a `## Remaining Boundary Matrix` (`Lane: <name> | classification: <token>`), and refuses it when any lane is self-classified `runnable`/`preauthorized-runnable`/`approved` — a runnable lane means the goal stays `active` and continues that lane instead of marking the whole goal blocked (the false-handoff bug where one blocked GitHub-publication boundary stranded a preauthorized `ceal-dev` HOTL apply/restart lane). Guarded by `tests/quality_gates/test_goal_artifact_blocked_matrix.py` and the resolution critique `charness-artifacts/critique/2026-06-18-issue-385-achieve-blocked-boundary-matrix.md`; a high-leverage fresh-eye causal review confirmed the workflow-boundary root cause and a 3-angle + counterweight resolution critique bundled the create-path guard. `check_requested_review_gate.py` blocks publish when a configured requested-review command fails or when the release record says review was unavailable without an explicit review waiver.

`publish_release_helpers.backend_command` now enforces a per-op placeholder allowlist at runtime: `release_view` accepts `{tag}`, `release_create` accepts `{tag}` and `{title}`, and `auth_check` accepts none. `fresh_checkout_probes` is now part of the release adapter contract: `current_release.py` reports whether probes are configured, and `publish_release.py --execute` runs declared probes in a temporary fresh clone after the release commit and before tag push or release creation.

`publish_release.py --execute --close-issue <number>` now preflights `gh issue view` before release mutation, puts close keywords in the direct release commit body, verifies GitHub issue state after push and release creation, records the carrier/manual-fallback evidence, and performs a manual close only if auto-close did not close the issue.

`publish_release.py --execute` now re-reads the public release after `gh release create` and records a post-publish artifact commit when the committed release record needs the verified release URL/status.

Release publish now fails closed when `git diff --name-only <base>..HEAD` cannot compute unreleased paths, before dry-run output or execute-mode version bumps, quality proof, artifacts, commits, tags, pushes, or release creation.
Release publish now also fails closed when configured real-host proof triggers are broken or the proof builder cannot run, while no-trigger repos can still publish without a surfaces manifest.

Release publish now fails closed when previous-tag base-ref lookup or fetch fails, preventing already-pushed release-surface changes from being hidden by an `origin/<branch>` fallback before dry-run output or execute mutation.

Release publish now treats post-create public release verification failure as checked failure instead of pending state: it retries `release view`, writes and pushes a failed-verification recovery artifact, exits nonzero, and skips issue closeout.

`publish_release.py --publish-current` now fails closed when local or remote release tag-history discovery fails while still allowing the explicit no-previous-release-tags case after both discovery commands succeed.

Release publish now runs focused release-adapter preflight before version bump or release artifact mutation when a supported release adapter path changed since the previous release tag. Real-host checklist changes replay `test_release_real_host.py`, fallback adapter paths are recognized, a failing focused preflight leaves the packaging manifest unchanged, and the release artifact records the adapter preflight status, changed fields, commands, or not-evaluable/not-configured non-claim.
